title: Aspidium gymnogrammoides
intro: Aspidium gymnogrammoides, also known as the Indian feather fern or maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium gymnogrammoides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Aspidium gymnogrammoides is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
